reason: The market is exhibiting clear signs of profit-taking and distribution, with Bitcoin struggling to sustain gains above key psychological levels like $70,000 and experiencing swift sell-offs after reaching $74,000. Negative developments in traditional finance (BlackRock fund issues) are directly impacting crypto prices, suggesting broader market contagion risk. Ongoing regulatory scrutiny (Binance probe) adds to systemic uncertainty. Investors are advised to exercise extreme caution as current market dynamics indicate a high likelihood of further downside pressure rather than sustained upward momentum.
headlines: Why bitcoin couldn't hold $70,000 despite its best week of Wall Street news in months
Managing financial AI agents is the only skill you’ll need to survive the AI layoffs
BlackRock private credit fund is latest to crack, hitting crypto prices and DeFi markets
Bitcoin buyers are cashing out fast after short-lived jump to $74,000
Binance tells Senate probe no accounts sent crypto directly to Iran
